title: chenopodium baccatum subsp. baccatum
intro: Chenopodium baccatum subsp. baccatum, also known as Tomatillo or Tomatl, is a perennial plant native to the Andean region in South America, belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which can help treat various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Chenopodium baccatum subsp. baccatum include its ability to reduce blood pressure, improve digestion, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
